overall.description: A cinematic tracking shot from the game Cyberpunk 2077 follows a black armored van driving through a foggy, futuristic urban plaza with modern glass architecture. The scene features golden morning light filtering through haze, with the camera smoothly tracking the vehicle as it passes a guarded building entrance and continues toward a distinctive circular structure in the distance.

scene_state.functional_zone: futuristic corporate plaza entrance with covered walkway

events.description: armed security guard becomes visible standing near the building entrance
camera begins moving laterally to track alongside the van as it approaches

agents.ego_description: driving slowly forward through the plaza in a black armored van with Rayfield branding

notable_agents.action: standing guard near the entrance holding a rifle

entered.detail: tall skyscraper with reflective glass facade and geometric patterns
large illuminated advertising screen mounted on the building exterior
distinctive multi-level circular structure with curved rooflines and exposed walkways
large rectangular planter containing green tropical plants

segments.scene_description: The segment opens with a black armored van approaching through a foggy futuristic plaza. The camera faces the front of the vehicle as it moves toward a corporate entrance flanked by a modern glass tower. A security guard stands watch on the left side as the van passes by, with a distinctive circular building visible in the hazy distance.

scene_state.functional_zone: open urban street with elevated transit structures

events.description: van body temporarily blocks view of background architecture as camera moves to side angle
van begins accelerating slightly and moving out of frame to the left

agents.ego_description: continuing down the street with camera tracking the left side of the vehicle, revealing detailed body panels and tinted windows

segments.scene_description: The camera transitions to a side-tracking shot following the van as it drives along the open street. The security guard and initial building entrance pass out of view behind the camera. The modern glass tower shifts to the center of the frame while the circular destination structure grows larger in the distance.

scene_state.functional_zone: open plaza approaching circular landmark building

events.description: van exits frame left, revealing open plaza ahead with parked vehicles and distant architecture
camera approaches parked beige sedan and the circular building becomes more prominent in the frame

agents.ego_description: moving forward through the plaza toward the circular building, passing parked vehicles on the right

entered.detail: vintage-style beige sedan with boxy 80s design parked along the curb
multi-level highway overpass with support columns visible through the fog

segments.scene_description: With the van now gone, the camera continues moving forward through the open plaza toward the distinctive circular building. A parked beige sedan comes into view on the right side as the camera approaches. The elevated highway structure becomes visible in the background on the right, while the initial glass tower recedes behind the camera.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement through a foggy futuristic plaza that shifts from tracking a moving vehicle to an independent glide toward a distant architectural landmark. It begins facing a black armored van as it approaches a corporate entrance flanked by a modern glass tower and a watchful security guard. As the van passes, the camera transitions into a side-tracking motion, leaving the guard behind while the glass tower shifts to the center of the frame and a distinctive circular building grows larger in the haze. The scene then opens further as the van exits the frame, allowing the camera to move forward alone toward the circular structure. Finally, the perspective passes a parked beige sedan on the right as an elevated highway emerges in the background and the initial glass tower recedes into the distance.
